name: Almond Pastry Fingers
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the shortening into flour; gradually add until a soft dough forms.
2. Add salt and enough water to achieve a consistency that is easy to work with.
3. Roll the dough very thin, then cut it into strips measuring approximately 4 inches by 3 inches.
4. Place each strip with about half to one teaspoon of filling.
5. Roll each filled strip lengthwise to create finger-shaped pastries.
6. Use a fork to seal the edges of each pastry.
7. Fry the pastries in vegetable oil until golden brown, then drain on absorbent paper.
8. Immediately dip the hot pastries into the syrup and let them cool.
This recipe makes 100-120 pieces. The almond pastries can be frozen uncooked or cooked as needed. Any leftover syrup can be stored in a refrigerator-safe jar and will not spoil.
